steps.title: Find the antecedent
steps.content: The blank refers back to "many even-toed ungulates," which is plural.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Rule: the pronoun must be plural (matching "many even-toed ungulates") and must work as an object — "prevent ___ from joining the ranks of the extinct."

A — "the animal" Singular. "Many even-toed ungulates" is plural, so a singular pronoun misrepresents the group. Fails.

B — "these animals" Plural, non-possessive. Correctly refers back to "many even-toed ungulates" and fills the object slot: "prevent these animals from joining." Correct.

C — "this animal's" Singular and possessive. Both problems apply: it mismatches number and a possessive form cannot serve as a direct object here. Fails.

D — "these animals'" Plural but possessive. A possessive cannot be the object of "prevent"; you cannot say "prevent these animals' from joining." Fails.

B is correct.
